conbody.p: Decay and impermanence are atmospheric themes of Halloween that emphasize the breakdown of physical structures and the passage of time. These themes reflect the gradual deterioration that accompanies aging, abandonment, and death.

section.title: Psychological Context
section.p: Signs of decay signal neglect, danger, or loss of control, triggering unease. Humans often associate deterioration with vulnerability and instability.
Halloween imagery uses decay to suggest that time has disrupted normal order.

section.title: Symbolic Meaning
section.p: Symbolically, decay represents impermanence and the inevitability of change. Crumbling structures, broken objects, and weathered surfaces serve as reminders that nothing remains intact indefinitely.
In Halloween contexts, decay is often stylized to evoke atmosphere rather than realism.

section.title: Decay in Modern Halloween Traditions
section.p: Modern decorations frequently depict aged or abandoned environments through cracked surfaces, artificial wear, and muted colors. These elements contribute to a sense of history and neglect.
Decay is commonly paired with symbols such as graveyards, spiderwebs, and haunted houses to reinforce the passage of time.

example.title: Halloween Elements That Reflect Decay and Impermanence
